expectations.evidence: Only Use Cases 1 (Post-Purchase) and 6 (Support Context) explicitly call all three layers (smartDigest/recall + smartGuidelines + prompt). Use Case 2 (Cart Abandonment) uses smartRecall and prompt but has no smartGuidelines call. Use Case 3 (Semantic Recommendations) uses smartDigest + smartRecall + prompt but no governance. Use Case 5 (Search) has no governance call. Use Case 7 (Replenishment) has no governance call. Governance is defined in Phase 3 as a separate section, but most individual proposals do not explicitly use all three layers.

All use cases generate written content via ai.prompt: Use Case 1 produces email_subject and email_body, Use Case 2 generates personalized recovery emails with abandonment reasoning, Use Case 3 writes recommendation explanations, Use Case 4 generates personalized win-back messages, Use Case 5 creates personalized search messages, Use Case 6 writes support briefs with recommended resolutions, Use Case 7 generates reorder nudge copy. AI writes content, not just product tags or selections.

No explicit differentiation test sections. Use Case 2 (Cart Abandonment) does not use governance, so its personalization is essentially memory recall + LLM generation, which could be approximated with Klaviyo data + an LLM call. Use Case 3 (Semantic Recommendations) is semantic search + LLM, close to a recommendation engine with an LLM wrapper. While the unified memory architecture adds value, individual proposals do not clearly demonstrate why Klaviyo segments + a basic LLM call would be insufficient. The response never directly addresses this differentiation.

Phase 1 explicitly maps all four sources (Shopify, browsing analytics, Klaviyo, loyalty program) with code for each ingestion. The architecture diagram shows all sources flowing into unified memory. The Data Sync Cadence table covers all sources plus support tickets. Use Case 4 (Lifecycle Campaigns) combines purchase history, engagement patterns, browsing behavior, and loyalty data. The unified memory concept is clear: 'ingests data from every source, extracts behavioral and preference insights using AI, and makes them available for personalized generation.'

Phase 3 defines three governance guidelines (Brand Voice, Discount Policy, Personalization Ethics) with detailed rules including discount caps, personalization boundaries, and frequency limits. However, governance is relegated to a separate phase rather than integrated into each proposal. Of 7 use cases, only Use Cases 1 and 6 explicitly call smartGuidelines in their code. Use Cases 2, 3, 4, 5, and 7 generate customer-facing content without any governance call. The governance role is described but not consistently applied across proposals.
